title: Paradigm File
description: Defines the complete paradigm table structure for one POS or sub-type, including matching rules, tag variables, fallbacks, and sections.

$schema.description: URI of this JSON Schema for validation.

pos.description: Primary POS tag this paradigm handles (e.g. "N", "V", "A").

match.description: Regex pattern tested against the full reading to select this paradigm. Uses a restricted PCRE-compatible subset (no lookbehind, no named groups).

exceptions.description: Ordered list of exception rules. Each exception has a match regex and a use field naming another paradigm file. First match wins.

tagVariables.description: Template variables extracted from the reading. Referenced in cell tag sequences as $varName. Each variable maps to an ordered list of candidate tag values. Overrides same-named tagVariables from meta.json.

fallbacks.description: Ordered fallback strategies for failed generation. Tried in order; first success wins.

sections.description: The paradigm table structure: an ordered list of sections.

styleClass.description: One or more semantic style class labels. Implementations map these to visual presentation.

exception.description: An exception rule that overrides the base paradigm.

match.description: Regex pattern tested against the full reading.

use.description: Full relative path to the paradigm file to use instead (must include .json).

tagVariableValues.description: Ordered list of candidate tag values. The first boundary-matching tag in the reading is used.

fallback.description: A fallback strategy for failed generation.

replace.description: Substring in the generator input to replace.

append.description: String to append to the generator input.

styleClass.description: Style class(es) applied to forms produced by this fallback.

condition.description: A declarative condition that evaluates to true or false based on the reading, lemma, or paradigm data.

hasTags.description: True if any form in the paradigm data contains ALL of these tags.

lacksTags.description: True if NO form in the paradigm data contains ANY of these tags.

hasLemma.description: True if the lemma matches this regex.

anyOf.description: At least one condition must be true.

section.description: A named group of related tables.

title.description: Section heading (localization key or literal text).

validateRows.description: If true, flag rows where ALL cells fail to generate.

table.description: A table defining a grid of generated forms.

headers.description: Column headers (localization keys or literal text). Empty string for unlabeled columns.

validateRows.description: If true, flag rows where ALL cells fail to generate.

row.description: One horizontal line in a paradigm table.

label.description: Row label (localization key or literal text).

cells.description: Cell definitions. Each cell is a tag sequence string, an object with tagSequence and styleClass, or an array where the first element is the primary form and subsequent elements are additional forms in object format.

colspan.description: Span widths for each cell. Each element specifies how many columns the corresponding cell occupies.

cell.description: A cell in a paradigm table. Either a simple tag sequence string, an object with tagSequence and styleClass, or an array where the first element is the primary form and subsequent elements are additional forms in object format.

oneOf.description: A tag sequence string appended to the lemma to produce a generator input.
A cell with additional forms. The first element is the primary form (string or object); subsequent elements are additional forms in object format.

cellObject.description: A cell with a tag sequence, a required style class, and an optional onlyIfGenerates flag.

tagSequence.description: Tag sequence appended to lemma to produce a generator input.

onlyIfGenerates.description: If true (the default), this cell is only included if the generator produces a form. If false, the cell is always included with a placeholder if generation fails.
